reviews.title: Nice hotel, a few issues
reviews.text: Our trip brought us to Arlo Chicago for two nights. We could reach most plans without thinking too hard about transportation. Housekeeping did a strong job; towels, counters and floors were consistently clean. Wi-Fi stayed reliable during video calls and evening browsing. It worked for this trip, though a few details kept it from being excellent.

reviews.title: Nice hotel, a few issues
reviews.text: I chose Arlo Chicago because I wanted to be in Chicago Loop. The location is fun, but the room was not as quiet as I hoped. I noticed clean linens and a tidy bathroom throughout the stay. The bed was acceptable, but the room felt cramped once two bags were open. It worked for this trip, though a few details kept it from being excellent.

reviews.title: Strong city stay
reviews.text: We booked Arlo Chicago for a short Chicago stay. The hotel details, including fitness center, restaurant, room service and free WiFi, gave the stay a polished feel. Compared with similar hotels nearby, the value felt solid. We heard hallway doors and nearby nightlife more than expected. It was an easy hotel to recommend for this kind of trip.

reviews.title: Worked well for this trip
reviews.text: I stayed at Arlo Chicago during a business trip. The hotel delivered enough comfort and convenience to feel like good value. Room comfort was mixed because the bed was fine but the space felt squeezed. I had no trouble using the Wi-Fi from the room or lobby. Morning food options were not the highlight, especially if you want variety.
